metadata.description: Managed-autonomy workflow for Claude Code: 11-step loop with audit/critique skills, four-lens parallel staff-review, diff-focused security + a11y reviews (with per-diff early-exit on docs-only PRs), agent-isolated planner + docs helpers, project-config-driven ship + ship-spike pipelines with stale-base preflight gate. v1.5.3 makes the PR `## Test plan` a non-forgeable projection of the /flow:verify-build findings buffer — /flow:ship Step 7 runs skills/ship/lib/render-test-plan.py to render one checkbox per Spec-walk criterion whose state IS the machine aggregated_verdict (PASS → checked, with the adversarial fresh-context judge's two-quote evidence; FAIL/Unknown → unchecked, with the judge's reason), plus the not_tested[] checklist — so the human confirms-and-merges instead of re-verifying, and the agent cannot show a criterion green without a real PASS. Skip / no-buffer / stale-buffer (buffer branch+sha ≠ current HEAD) / malformed → an honest 'no behavioral gate ran (reason); manual verification required' fallback, never a forged or stale render (so a platform:library repo — including flow's own — always takes the fallback). Renderer behavior pinned by evals/run_render_evals.py; attests behavioral/text verification only (not visual — Deliverable-quality V2) and only over DECLARED Spec-walk criteria (under-declaration coverage is a queued follow-up). v1.5.2 makes doc-currency automatic in the ship pipeline — /flow:ship Step 5a reconciles roadmap 'Now' + plan 'Current Focus' to the shipped version (Recently-shipped line, next-up pointer, FB-reservation clear) and Step 5b mechanically blocks any ship whose forward-looking docs don't reference the current version (enforced in-pipeline, NOT via manual /flow:doctor, which keeps a secondary Check 2.6 mirror); also corrects docs/upgrade.md (one `/plugin marketplace update` command updates the installed plugin, and autoUpdate automates it). v1.5.1 adds a `Visual-walk` plan field — declared, checkable visual/UX acceptance criteria a plan states for UI changes (gated on the uiSurface slot; N/A under spike/tiny), giving the Step 8/9 visual dial-in a declared bar and closing the workflow.md 'declared visual criteria' dangling reference; declaration-only. v1.5.0 adds ship-time gate semantics: security/a11y BLOCKERs carry a resolution-confidence tag ([auto-fixable] | [decision-required], default decision-required when unsure); decision-required findings and non-converging verify-build regressions route to a draft PR + a pinned NOT READY TO MERGE manifest (never a silent proceed, never a hard mid-loop halt), so an unresolved blocker reaches the human at the merge gate; verify-build at ship is reframed as a confirmation re-run (discovery moves to the Step 8/9 readiness boundary; visual sign-off folds into the merge gate). The v1.4.0 auto-advance predicate is unchanged (still requires a verify-build PASS; FB-0018 invariant intact); only ship-internal failure handling changed. It also makes the reviewer skills (/flow:audit-plan, /flow:audit-completion, /flow:critique-plan) and /flow:ship-spike model-invocable (disable-model-invocation false) — the only two human gates are plan approval and merge, and no review or ship-spike skill is itself a gate (superseding the v1.4.0 note that ship-spike stays manual); the context:fork reviewers rely on the v1.4.2 session-discovery fix to resolve worktree transcripts. v1.4.2 fixes a session-transcript discovery bug in extract_session.py — the cwd→project-dir encoding now matches Claude Code (all non-alphanumerics map to '-', not just '/') and discovery prefers the exact CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, so the audit/critique reviewers no longer silently run context-starved from worktree or dotted-path sessions. v1.4.1 makes /flow:ship + /flow:ship-spike write a per-step `## Flow run` PR body that documents the full loop run (each step ✓ ran / skipped-with-reason + any notable decision or finding; routine steps show '—', no manufactured filler), replacing the generic Reviews blurb. v1.4.0 makes /flow:ship auto-invocable: the agent auto-advances from Step 8 into the ship pipeline when the ship-readiness predicate holds (all spec-walk checkboxes checked, no open BLOCKER, no unresolved MEDIUM/LOW assumption, /flow:verify-build would return PASS) and the FB-0011 risk gate is clear — collapsing the autonomous loop to its two load-bearing human gates (plan approval, merge). Skipped-verify cases (library/none platform, doc-only diff) and spike mode stay manual; ship still never merges. v1.2.6 adds bounded-retry mechanical preflight (new `preflightCmd` slot; Step 1c of /flow:ship + /flow:ship-spike; N≤3 retries with diff-hash oscillation detection; loops only on externally-verifiable exit codes — reviewer outputs at Step 2 stay single-pass). v1.2.5 sharpens the reviewer pipeline along adversarial dimensions: auditor + plan-critic gain a prove-or-disprove self-check that drops findings without specific evidence; lens-staff-engineer reads adversarially ('assume the diff is broken'); /flow:security-review shifts to red-team identity ('find an exploitable vulnerability') with a trace-input-source disprove step.

plugins.name: flow
plugins.description: Managed-autonomy 11-step loop. Bundles the skeptical reviewers (auditor + plan-critic), the four-lens parallel staff-review (engineer / UX-designer / design-engineer / push-further), diff-focused /flow:security-review + /flow:accessibility-review, /flow:verify-build (runtime behavioral verification gate — v1.3.0), /flow:audit-coverage (under-declaration coverage auditor — v1.6.0), /flow:ship + /flow:ship-spike pipelines, /flow:workflow-help, /flow:doctor verification skill, planner + docs context-isolation agents, portable rules (general, plan-discipline, documentation, exploration), memory-corpus tool, flow.config.json JSON Schema (22 slots), and default hooks. v1.6.1 adds rendered visual capture + an ephemeral HTML walkthrough to /flow:verify-build (Deliverable-quality track V2/V3a): for each declared `Visual-walk` state, flow captures and persists a screenshot itself — a11y-gated (snapshot the a11y tree, assert the intended state, THEN screenshot), driving the platform's screenshot MCP (XcodeBuildMCP returns a native frame path on iOS; bundled /verify only narrates frames to the fresh-context judges) — plus an a11y_snapshot for text/status, writing path-referenced observations; two additive findings-buffer fields land (criteria[].grounding rationale + top-level open_questions; schema_version stays 1.0); the rubric judges visual claims pairwise-vs-baseline (no baseline ⇒ Unknown) and reads text from the a11y tree; a stdlib-Python renderer emits one self-contained HTML report — the artifact the human opens at the merge gate — with grounding callouts, per-dimension verdict cards, a standalone 'Open questions for you' block, and a 'what we did NOT test' checklist; an open_questions[this-iteration] entry blocks Step 8 auto-advance. New verifyReportPath slot. Validated by a cold skill-driven /flow:verify-build run on a real iOS surface (round 1 caught 3 §5a prose gaps, fixed + FB-0050; round 2 green). v1.6.0 adds /flow:audit-coverage — a coverage auditor closing the under-declaration hole the v1.5.3 Test-plan render left: it compares the workspace diff against the declared **Spec-walk:** criteria and flags behavior changes no criterion covers (a behavior /flow:verify-build never tested, so the rendered Test plan would be honestly all-green while the change ships unverified). Each gap routes to the draft manifest (decision-required) — PR mechanically NOT-READY until the criterion is declared + verified or the human waives it; never auto-added, never a hard halt. Reuses the auditor agent via a new 'Undeclared change' category (coverage mode) + extract-criteria.py; runs at the Step 8 readiness boundary + as the fourth /flow:ship Step 2 reviewer; runs on ALL platforms (does NOT skip on platform:library|none, unlike verify-build); self-skips on doc/test/refactor-only diffs or no **Spec-walk:** block. Best-effort LLM judgment — raises the completeness bar, does not deterministically guarantee it (false negatives possible). v1.5.3 makes the PR `## Test plan` a non-forgeable projection of the /flow:verify-build findings buffer — /flow:ship Step 7 runs skills/ship/lib/render-test-plan.py to render one checkbox per Spec-walk criterion whose state IS the machine aggregated_verdict (PASS → checked, with the adversarial fresh-context judge's two-quote evidence; FAIL/Unknown → unchecked, with the judge's reason), plus the not_tested[] checklist — so the human confirms-and-merges instead of re-verifying, and the agent cannot show a criterion green without a real PASS. Skip / no-buffer / stale-buffer (buffer branch+sha ≠ current HEAD) / malformed → an honest 'no behavioral gate ran (reason); manual verification required' fallback, never a forged or stale render (so a platform:library repo — including flow's own — always takes the fallback). Renderer behavior pinned by evals/run_render_evals.py; attests behavioral/text verification only (not visual — Deliverable-quality V2) and only over DECLARED Spec-walk criteria (under-declaration coverage is a queued follow-up). v1.5.2 makes doc-currency automatic in the ship pipeline — /flow:ship Step 5a reconciles roadmap 'Now' + plan 'Current Focus' to the shipped version (Recently-shipped line, next-up pointer, FB-reservation clear) and Step 5b mechanically blocks any ship whose forward-looking docs don't reference the current version (enforced in-pipeline, NOT via manual /flow:doctor, which keeps a secondary Check 2.6 mirror); also corrects docs/upgrade.md (one `/plugin marketplace update` command updates the installed plugin, and autoUpdate automates it). v1.5.1 adds a `Visual-walk` plan field — declared, checkable visual/UX acceptance criteria a plan states when the change has a UI surface (gated on the uiSurface slot; N/A under spike/tiny), giving the Step 8/9 visual dial-in a declared bar and closing the workflow.md 'declared visual criteria' dangling reference; declaration-only (mechanical verification is a later roadmap link). v1.5.0 adds ship-time gate semantics: security/a11y BLOCKERs carry a resolution-confidence tag ([auto-fixable] | [decision-required], default decision-required when unsure); decision-required findings and non-converging verify-build regressions route to a draft PR + a pinned NOT READY TO MERGE manifest (never a silent proceed, never a hard mid-loop halt), so an unresolved blocker reaches the human at the merge gate; verify-build at ship is reframed as a confirmation re-run (discovery moves to the Step 8/9 readiness boundary; visual sign-off folds into the merge gate). The v1.4.0 auto-advance predicate is unchanged (still requires a verify-build PASS; FB-0018 invariant intact); only ship-internal failure handling changed. It also makes the reviewer skills (/flow:audit-plan, /flow:audit-completion, /flow:critique-plan) and /flow:ship-spike model-invocable (disable-model-invocation false) — the only two human gates are plan approval and merge, and no review or ship-spike skill is itself a gate (superseding the v1.4.0 note that ship-spike stays manual); the context:fork reviewers rely on the v1.4.2 session-discovery fix to resolve worktree transcripts. v1.4.2 fixes a session-transcript discovery bug in extract_session.py — the cwd→project-dir encoding now matches Claude Code (all non-alphanumerics map to '-', not just '/') and discovery prefers the exact CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, so the audit/critique reviewers no longer silently run context-starved from worktree or dotted-path sessions. v1.4.1 makes /flow:ship + /flow:ship-spike write a per-step `## Flow run` PR body documenting the full loop run (each step ✓ ran / skipped-with-reason, mode- and config-dependent; Notable cell = genuine signal or '—', no manufactured filler; follow-ups stay canonical in roadmap/plan; PR still never merged), replacing the generic Reviews blurb. v1.4.0 makes /flow:ship auto-invocable (disable-model-invocation false): the agent auto-advances from Step 8 into ship when the ship-readiness predicate holds (spec-walk complete, no open BLOCKER, no unresolved MEDIUM/LOW assumption, verify-build PASS) and the FB-0011 risk gate is clear, else stops and presents; skipped-verify + spike stay manual; plan and merge gates untouched. v1.3.0 adds /flow:verify-build as the third final-pass reviewer at /flow:ship Step 2 (alongside security + a11y). Wraps bundled /verify with plan-driven criteria extraction from **Spec-walk:** checkboxes, fresh-context adversarial transformation, per-dimension parallel judges (PASS/FAIL/Unknown with two-citation evidence), Unknown-blocking gate per FB-0011, and structured findings buffer routed to /flow:ship Step 4a for FB-XXXX synthesis. Closes the static-analysis-only gap in the loop's verification surface (Potemkin-interface / hallucinated-success class no static reviewer catches). Inherits FB-0012 bounded-retry contract: judge runs single-pass; budget cap forces Unknown on overrun (mechanical exit signal — no judge-output loop); reward-hacking guards baked into adversarial.md. 4 new schema slots (platform / verifyEnabled / verifyFindingsPath / verifyBudgetCalls); ship-spike Step 2 spike-mode invocation; doctor Check 5.3 surfaces missing /run-skill-generator recipes. v1.2.6 adds bounded-retry mechanical preflight to /flow:ship + /flow:ship-spike (new `preflightCmd` slot at Step 1c; on non-zero exit, fix-and-retry up to N=3 invocations with diff-hash oscillation detection; loops ONLY on externally-verifiable exit codes per Anthropic's evaluator-optimizer guidance — reviewer outputs at Step 2 stay deliberately single-pass to prevent the reward-hacking failure mode that loops over LLM-judgment outputs invite). v1.2.5 sharpens the reviewer pipeline along adversarial dimensions: auditor + plan-critic gain a prove-or-disprove self-check (findings must survive an attempt to cite the specific session text or third citation that would invalidate them); lens-staff-engineer reads adversarially ('assume the diff is broken — what's the most likely break?') for higher correctness recall; /flow:security-review shifts to red-team identity ('find an exploitable vulnerability') with a trace-input-source-back disprove step. Plan-critic also gains fan-out hunt vocabulary in Internal incoherence (PR-G FOLLOW-UP #5). v1.2.3 adds consistency discipline (FB-0010): the lens-staff-engineer agent now explicitly hunts fan-out contradictions (counts/names/slots referenced in N places, only some updated) and silent-skip patterns (failures swallowed via `2>/dev/null` / unset-fallback / regex inversion); /flow:doctor gains Check 2.5 comparing documented slot counts against the schema source-of-truth; workflow.md Step 4 names the discipline.
